top_insights.headline: Month-to-month contracts drive 89% of all churn
top_insights.detail: 1,655 of 1,869 churned customers are on month-to-month contracts (42.7% churn rate vs. 2.8% on two-year).

top_insights.headline: Fiber optic customers churn 2.2× more than DSL customers
top_insights.detail: Fiber optic: 41.9% churn vs DSL: 19.0%. Despite higher ARPU, fiber customers are the highest-volume churners (1,297 customers lost).
top_insights.action: Free tech support + security bundle for fiber customers

top_insights.headline: 56% of new customers (0-6 months) churn — the critical danger window
top_insights.detail: 661 of 1,181 customers with 0-6 month tenure have churned. Churn rate drops from 56% to 9.2% for customers with 49+ months.
top_insights.action: 90-day structured onboarding program with personal check-ins

top_insights.headline: $84,696/month in revenue at immediate risk from 1,491 high-risk customers
top_insights.detail: Equivalent to $1,016,346/year. These customers have been flagged by the Risk Model as 'High Risk' based on contract, payment, and tenure signals.
top_insights.action: Dedicated retention agent outreach within 14 days

top_insights.headline: Churned customers paid $13.17/month MORE than retained customers
top_insights.detail: Churned ARPU: $74.44/mo vs. Active ARPU: $61.27/mo. This indicates a value perception problem — customers don't feel they're getting their money's worth.
top_insights.action: Value demonstration program: ROI reports & service utilization coaching

by_contract.observation: Month-to-month contracts have a 42.7% churn rate — 15x higher than two-year contracts at 2.8%.
by_contract.impact: Month-to-month churners account for 88.5% of all churned customers (1,655 of 1,869).
by_contract.recommendation: Offer a 15-20% discount for customers who switch from month-to-month to annual contracts in their first 90 days.

by_payment.observation: Electronic check customers churn at 45.3% — nearly 3x higher than auto-payment customers.
by_payment.impact: 1,071 electronic check churners represent the single largest payment-method revenue loss group.
by_payment.recommendation: Incentivize auto-payment enrollment with a 5% monthly discount. Target electronic check users proactively.

by_internet.observation: Fiber optic customers churn at 41.9% — more than twice the rate of DSL customers at 19%.
by_internet.impact: Fiber customers generate higher ARPU but leave faster, creating a value perception problem.
by_internet.recommendation: Add tech support and online security bundles for fiber customers at no extra cost for first 6 months.

by_tenure.observation: Churn risk drops dramatically with tenure. Customers 0-6 months old churn at 56% — the highest risk window.
by_tenure.impact: Customers surviving past 24 months have a churn rate under 19%, making early retention critical.
by_tenure.recommendation: Invest in a 90-day onboarding program with personal check-ins at days 30, 60, and 90.

by_senior.observation: Senior citizens churn at 41.7% versus 23.6% for non-seniors — a 76% higher relative risk.
by_senior.impact: 476 senior churners at higher average charges represent significant premium-segment revenue loss.
by_senior.recommendation: Create a dedicated Senior Value Plan with simplified billing, priority support, and loyalty discounts.

by_gender.observation: Gender has virtually no impact on churn — rates are 26.2% (male) vs 26.9% (female).
by_gender.impact: Gender-based segmentation should NOT be prioritized for retention campaigns.
by_gender.recommendation: Redirect budget from gender-based targeting to contract-type and payment-method interventions.

charges_distribution.observation: Churned customers are heavily concentrated in the $70-$90+ range — high spenders who leave.
charges_distribution.impact: Churned ARPU ($74.44) exceeds active ARPU ($61.27) — customers pay more but still leave.
charges_distribution.recommendation: Value perception is the problem, not price. Focus on demonstrating ROI for high-charge customers.

risk_patterns.pattern: High charges + Low engagement + Short tenure
Fiber optic + No tech support + Short tenure (<12 mo)
Month-to-month + Electronic check + No online security
Paperless billing + Electronic check + No online backup

segments.name: Loyal Customers
segments.recommendation: Reward loyalty with exclusive perks, priority support, and referral bonuses. These are brand ambassadors — leverage them for testimonials and word-of-mouth marketing. Offer upgrade paths to premium services at discounted rates.

segments.name: At-Risk Customers
segments.description: Customers flagged as high risk by the risk model
segments.recommendation: Immediate intervention required. Assign dedicated retention agents for personal outreach. Offer contract incentives, service upgrades, or billing credits. Investigate and resolve any service issues proactively.

segments.name: High-Value Customers
segments.recommendation: White-glove service with dedicated account managers. Offer exclusive loyalty programs, early access to new features, and premium support. Prevent competitor poaching with value-locked benefits.

segments.name: Low-Value Customers
segments.recommendation: Focus on engagement and upselling. Send personalized offers for service bundles. Educate on value of additional services. Use automated nurture campaigns with progressive discount offers.

segments.name: New Customers
segments.description: Recently onboarded customers in their first 6 months
segments.recommendation: Intensive onboarding program: welcome calls, guided setup, 30/60/90-day check-ins. Provide self-service resources and community forums. Offer a '6-month satisfaction guarantee' to build trust and reduce early churn.

segments.name: Dormant Customers
segments.description: Low engagement customers with minimal service usage
segments.recommendation: Launch re-engagement campaigns with compelling offers. Show value of unused services through personalized demos. Consider 'win-back' pricing for service additions. Schedule proactive check-in calls.

segments.name: Long-Term Subscribers
segments.description: Customers with over 3 years of tenure
segments.recommendation: Recognize their loyalty with milestone rewards (3-year, 5-year anniversaries). Offer them beta access to new services. Create a loyalty tier system with increasing benefits. Prevent complacency — don't take them for granted.

segments.name: Premium Customers
segments.recommendation: Offer premium bundle discounts to lock in their full service suite. Provide concierge-level support. Cross-sell complementary services. Create 'all-in-one' plans with price advantage over à la carte pricing.

recommendations.title: Contract Upgrade Campaign
recommendations.description: Offer 15-20% discount for month-to-month customers switching to annual contracts in their first 90 days.
recommendations.evidence: Month-to-month contracts have 15x the churn rate of two-year contracts. Contract commitment is the #1 retention driver.

recommendations.title: Auto-Payment Migration Drive
recommendations.description: Incentivize electronic check users to switch to automatic bank transfer or credit card with a 5% monthly discount.
recommendations.target_segment: Electronic check users (2,365 customers, 45.3% churn rate)
recommendations.evidence: Electronic check customers churn at 45.3% vs. 16% for auto-payment customers. Friction in payment correlates strongly with disengagement.

recommendations.title: 90-Day New Customer Onboarding Program
recommendations.description: Structured 3-touch onboarding: welcome call (day 1), setup check-in (day 30), value review (day 90).
recommendations.target_segment: New customers 0-6 months tenure (1,181 customers, 56% churn rate)
recommendations.evidence: 56% of customers in their first 6 months churn. Churn drops to 9.2% for customers past 4 years. Early intervention is highest-ROI.

recommendations.title: Fiber Optic Value Bundle
recommendations.description: Offer free Online Security + Tech Support for 6 months to all fiber optic customers on month-to-month contracts.
recommendations.target_segment: Fiber optic + month-to-month customers (highest risk pattern, ~65% churn rate)
recommendations.evidence: Fiber optic customers churn at 41.9% vs 7.4% for no-internet. Service add-ons reduce perceived price-to-value gap.

recommendations.title: High-Risk Customer Retention Task Force
recommendations.description: Assign dedicated retention agents to personally outreach to 1,491 flagged high-risk customers within 14 days.
recommendations.evidence: $1,016,346/year in revenue is at risk from high-risk segment alone. Proactive outreach can reduce churn by 20-30% within this segment.

portfolio.task: Future Interns — Data Science & Analytics — Task 2 (2026)

pipeline_steps.name: Data Cleaning
pipeline_steps.description: Handle missing TotalCharges, standardize categorical values, remove duplicates

pipeline_steps.name: Feature Engineering
pipeline_steps.description: Create ServiceCount, EngagementScore, CLV_Estimate, CustomerHealthIndex, RetentionScore, RiskCategory

pipeline_steps.name: Exploratory Data Analysis
pipeline_steps.description: Statistical analysis of churn by contract, payment, internet, tenure, demographics

pipeline_steps.name: Advanced Analytics
pipeline_steps.description: Kaplan-Meier survival analysis, KPI calculations, distribution analysis

pipeline_steps.name: Customer Segmentation
pipeline_steps.description: 8 business-logic segments with churn rates, revenue, and action recommendations

pipeline_steps.name: Cohort Analysis
pipeline_steps.description: Quarterly cohort retention matrix, revenue by cohort, year-over-year trends

pipeline_steps.name: Churn Intelligence
pipeline_steps.description: Cramér's V and point-biserial correlations to rank churn drivers, behavioral risk patterns

pipeline_steps.name: ML Churn Prediction
pipeline_steps.description: Logistic Regression (AUC: 84.44%) and Random Forest (AUC: 84.09%) with 5-fold cross-validation
